g.data-narration: Informed Search Strategies use heuristic functions to guide the search toward goals efficiently, unlike blind search methods that explore without any knowledge of the problem domain.
A heuristic function h(n) estimates the cost from node n to a goal state, providing domain-specific knowledge to guide search.
g.text: Heuristic: estimates cost from current node to goal. Common examples for 8-puzzle:
• h1: Misplaced Tiles - count tiles not in goal position
• h2: Manhattan Distance - sum of distances to goal positions

g.data-narration: Greedy Best-First Search expands the node with lowest h(n) value to reach the goal quickly. It is fast but neither complete in infinite spaces nor guaranteed to find optimal solutions.
g.text: Expands node with LOWEST h(n) - appears closest to goal.
• Not complete in infinite spaces (can loop)
• Not optimal - may miss shortest path

g.data-narration: A* Search combines g(n) path cost with h(n) heuristic estimate using f(n) = g(n) + h(n) to find optimal solutions. It is complete and optimal when the heuristic is admissible.
g.text: Evaluation: f(n) = g(n) + h(n) where g(n)=path cost, h(n)=estimated cost to goal
• Complete if branching factor is finite
• OPTIMAL when h(n) is admissible (never overestimates)

g.data-narration: An admissible heuristic never overestimates true cost: h(n) ≤ h*(n), making it optimistic. A consistent heuristic satisfies the triangle inequality h(n) ≤ c + h(n') for all successors.
g.text: Admissible: h(n) ≤ h*(n) - never overestimates true cost (optimistic)
Consistent: h(n) ≤ c + h(n') for all successors - stronger than admissible
Dominance: h1 dominates h2 if h1(n) ≥ h2(n) for all states

g.data-narration: Example: In the 8-puzzle problem, Manhattan Distance dominates Misplaced Tiles because each tile must move at least its Manhattan distance from its goal position to reach the solution.

Greedy Best-First Search finds solutions quickly but produces suboptimal paths. A* Search guarantees optimal solutions when using admissible heuristics, though it requires more memory and time to explore nodes.
